description: Graphics, elements, and functionality designed for supporting the IsoRealms Spindizzy remake.

CameraGameplay.description: A camera shows a single zone at a time, with ability to view the zone from different isometric angles.

InitialAngle.name: Initial Angle
InitialAngle.description: Angle at which this camera is initially set.

RotateDuration.name: Rotate Duration
RotateDuration.description: The time it takes to rotate the camera from one angle to another (in ms).

CameraLinked.description: A camera copies the state of another view's camera.

CameraOverview.description: A camera that shows an entire world from above.

CameraTransitional.description: A camera that transitions between two other configured cameras.

Duration.name: Duration
Duration.description: The time it takes to transition between the two states (in ms).

OnDepartureFromStart.name: On Departure from State A
OnDepartureFromStart.description: Action to perform when this camera leaves state A.

OnArriveAtEnd.name: On Arrival at State B
OnArriveAtEnd.description: Action to perform when this camera reaches state B.

OnDepartureFromEnd.name: On Departure from State B
OnDepartureFromEnd.description: Action to perform when this camera leaves state B.

OnArrivalFromStart.name: On Arrival at State A
OnArrivalFromStart.description: Action to perform when this camera reaches state A.

CameraVariant.description: A camera that combines the states of other cameras.

Location.name: Location
Location.description: Use the location of the specified camera to position this camera.

Angle.name: Angle
Angle.description: Use the angle of the specified camera to rotate this camera.

Tilt.name: Tilt
Tilt.description: Use the tilt of the specified camera to tilt this camera.

Zoom.name: Zoom
Zoom.description: Use the zoom of the specified camera to zoom this camera.

SurfacePatternOutline.description: Renders outlines around surfacess to give them a cartoonish appearance.

SurfacePatternSplitVariant.description: Renders surfaces using different styles depending on whether the tiles are split into triangles.

Regular.name: Regular Pattern
Regular.description: Pattern to use for tiles without a split.

SplitA.name: Split A Pattern
SplitA.description: Pattern to use for one half of split tiles.

SplitB.name: Split B Pattern
SplitB.description: Pattern to use for other half of split tiles.

SurfacePatternTile.description: Renders surfaces using a single texture.

WallPatternCap.description: Renders walls with using different textures at the top, bottom, and middle sections.

Top.name: Top
Top.description: Texture applied to top part of walls.

Middle.name: Middle
Middle.description: Texture applied between top and bottom part of walls.

Bottom.name: Bottom
Bottom.description: Texture applied to bottom part of walls.

WallPatternOutline.description: Renders outlines around walls to give them a cartoonish appearance.

WallPatternTile.description: Renders walls using a single texture.

ZoneViewTypeOverview.description: Renders coloured squares to represent zones.

ZoneObjectTypeTraitBoundary.description: Applies a boundary to this entity.

ZoneObjectTypeTraitCellLocation.description: Applies a cell location to this entity.

ZoneObjectTypeTraitChaser.description: Applies a chase behaviour to this entity.

ZoneObjectTypeTraitModel.description: Applies a visual representation to this entity.

ZoneObjectTypeTraitMovable.description: Applies the ability to move to this entity.

ZoneObjectTypeTraitSpinner.description: Applies a visual representation to this entity that spins as it moves.

Alien.description: Physical entities that chases a specific target, capable of triggering collisions with other entities.

Appearance.name: Appearance
Appearance.description: Model to visually represent this type of alien.

Target.name: Target
Target.description: Target that aliens of this type will attempt to move towards

Acceleration.name: Acceleration
Acceleration.description: Rate at which the aliens of this type increase their movement speed

Friction.name: Friction
Friction.description: Rate at which acceleration drops off for aliens of this type.

SpinSpeed.name: Spin Speed
SpinSpeed.description: Speed at which aliens of this type spin as they move.  This is purely a visual effect.

Height.name: Height
Height.description: Height of aliens of this type in number of platform blocks.

Radius.name: Radius
Radius.description: Radius of aliens of this type in number of platform blocks.

HugThreshold.name: Hug Momentum Threshold
HugThreshold.description: Speed under which aliens of this type will stick to a wall and slide along it instead of bouncing off it.

Ball.description: Textures in the style of the C64 Spindizzy Ball craft.  These textures are designed to be applied to billboarded sprites.

Fill.name: Fill Colour
Fill.description: Main colour of this ball texture.

Shine.name: Shine Colour
Shine.description: Colour of the 'shine' on the upper part of this ball texture.

Outline.name: Outline Colour
Outline.description: Colour of the outline around the edge of this ball texture.

BoundaryHandler.description: Define actions to be performed when objects of a specific type enter or leave fixed boundaries of a specific type.  This can be used to define on players encountering different types of pick-ups, or upon entering and leaving zones.

Entity.name: Entity Type
Entity.description: Type of entity to react upon boundary entry and exit.

Boundary.name: Boundary Type
Boundary.description: Type of boundary to test entry and exit of.

OnEntry.name: Action on Entry
OnEntry.description: Action to perform each time an entity of the selected type enters a boundary of the selected type.

OnExit.name: Action on Exit
OnExit.description: Action to perform each time an entity of the selected type leaving a boundary of the selected type.

C64LiftGraphics.description: Sets of textures intended for use as lift elements, in the style of C64 Spindizzy.

Primary.name: Primary Colour
Primary.description: Primary colour used in this set of textures.

Secondary.name: Secondary Colour
Secondary.description: Secondary colour used in this set of textures.

Outline.name: Outline Colour
Outline.description: Outline colour used in this set of textures.

C64TerrainGraphics.description: Sets of texture intended to be applied to terrain elements, in the style of C64 Spindizzy.

Floor.name: Floor Colour
Floor.description: Colour mainly applied to textures to be used as floors.

Wall.name: Wall Colour
Wall.description: Colour mainly applied to textures to be used as walls.  Note that this colour is mixed with the floor texture to generate a second variation of the standard wall texture for displaying at a different angle.

Grid.name: Grid Colour
Grid.description: Colour mainly applied to the grid pattern used on walls and floors.

Highlight.name: Detail Colour
Highlight.description: Colour that adds extra variation to some textures.

CollisionHandler.description: Define actions to be performed when objects of one type collide with object of another type.  This can be used to apply damage to the player upon collision with alien crafts.

EntityA.name: Entity Type A
EntityA.description: React upon collisions between entities of this type and Entity Type B.

EntityB.name: Entity Type B
EntityB.description: React upon collisions between entities of this type and Entity Type A.

OnCollision.name: Action on Collision
OnCollision.description: Action to perform each time two entities of each respective type begin to overlap

OnSeparation.name: Action on Separation
OnSeparation.description: Action to perform each time two entities of each respective type cease to overlap.

DebrisChunk.description: Simple tetrahedron models that can be used to represent chunks of derbis from a destroyed craft.

Side1.name: Side 1 Colour
Side1.description: Colour of one side of this debris chunk model.

Side2.name: Side 2 Colour
Side2.description: Colour of one side of this debris chunk model.

Side3.name: Side 3 Colour
Side3.description: Colour of one side of this debris chunk model.

Side4.name: Side 4 Colour
Side4.description: Colour of one side of this debris chunk model.

Outline.name: Outline Colour
Outline.description: Colour of the outline of around the edge of this debris chunk model.

Gyroscope.description: Models in the style of the C64 Spindizzy Gyroscope craft.

Quadrant1.name: Quadrant 1 Colour
Quadrant1.description: Colour of one quadrant of this gyroscope model.

Quadrant2.name: Quadrant 2 Colour
Quadrant2.description: Colour of one quadrant of this gyroscope model.

Quadrant3.name: Quadrant 3 Colour
Quadrant3.description: Colour of one quadrant of this gyroscope model.

Quadrant4.name: Quadrant 4 Colour
Quadrant4.description: Colour of one quadrant of this gyroscope model.

Outline.name: Outline Colour
Outline.description: Colour of the outline of around the edge of this gyroscope model.

Jewel.description: Models in the style of C64 Spindizzy jewels, including colour changing panels.

PanelPhase.name: Panel Phase Colour
PanelPhase.description: Panels of this jewel model will phase through this colour.

Outline.name: Outline Colour
Outline.description: Outline colour of this jewel model.

Lift.description: Vertically moving platforms that enable players and other objects to reach higher places.

Appearance.name: Appearance
Appearance.description: Model to visually represent lifts of this type.

State.name: State
State.description: Determine whether all lifts of this type are moving or not.

OnMove.name: Action on Movement
OnMove.description: Action to perform upon movement of the lift.  The action is triggered each time the lift begins movement from or passes through a whole block height.  It is used to create the lift 'ticking' sound used in Spindizzy.

ModelCycler.description: Models that programmatically cycle through the appearances of other models.

Model.name: Model
Model.description: A model to cycle through within this set.

ModelAdd.name: Model
ModelAdd.description: Add another model to cycle through within this set.

PickUp.description: Fixed-place objects that can be collected by players or other objects.  Note that interaction between entity type and pick-up types can be defined in Boundary Handlers.

Appearance.name: Appearance
Appearance.description: Model to visually represent pick-ups of this type.

Player.description: Crafts whose movements are controlled through external inputs.

Radius.name: Radius
Radius.description: Radius of players of this type in number of platform blocks.

Height.name: Height
Height.description: Height of players of this type in number of platform blocks.

Acceleration.name: Acceleration
Acceleration.description: Rate at which the players of this type increase their movement speed

WallBounce.name: Wall Bounce
WallBounce.description: Factor of speed loss when bouncing off of walls.

StepReach.name: Step Reach
StepReach.description: Maximum height of steps that players of this type can climb.

HugThreshold.name: Hug Momentum Threshold
HugThreshold.description: Speed under which players of this type will stick to a wall and slide along it instead of bouncing off it.

Appearance.name: Appearance
Appearance.description: Model to visually represent this type of player.

SpinSpeed.name: Spin Speed
SpinSpeed.description: Speed at which players of this type spin as they move.  This is purely a visual effect.

InputX.name: Input X Movement
InputX.description: Input controlling movement of players of this type along the X axis.

InputY.name: Input Y Movement
InputY.description: Input controlling movement of players of this type along the Y axis.

InputThrust.name: Input Thrust
InputThrust.description: Input for doubling movement speed of players of this type.

ViewOrientation.name: View Orientation
ViewOrientation.description: Movement inputs are adjusted according to angle of the specified view.  This should be the view that is visible to the viewer controlling the player craft.

OnRespawn.name: Action on Respawn
OnRespawn.description: Action to perform upon respawning after being destroyed.

OnImpact.name: Action on Impact
OnImpact.description: Action to perform upon falling onto a floor surface that doesn't cause the player to bounce.

OnBounce.name: Action on Bounce
OnBounce.description: Action to perform upon falling onto a floor surface that causes the player to bounce.

OnWallBounce.name: Action on Wall Bounce
OnWallBounce.description: Action to perform upon bouncing from hitting a wall.

RespawnDelay.name: Respawn Delay
RespawnDelay.description: Amount of time in milliseconds after being destroyed before player craft respawns.

Terrain.description: Terrain is used for constructing fixed platforms, walls, water, and other shapes that the player and other objects can traverse.

SurfaceFriction.name: Surface Friction
SurfaceFriction.description: Rate at which objects moving along surfaces of this terrain type lose speed.

SurfaceGrip.name: Surface Grip
SurfaceGrip.description: Factor at which movement changes to objects moving along surfaces of this terrain type are applied.  A value of 1 provides normal control.  A value less than 1 makes surfaces more slippery and a value of 0 nullifies control of objects on the surface.

SurfaceBounce.name: Surface Bounce
SurfaceBounce.description: Factor at which objects hitting surfaces of this terrain type are propelled upwards.  A value of 1 causes objects to reach the same height as they fell from.  A value less than 1 causes objects to lose height on each bounce.  A value of 0 means that objects will not bounce when hitting this surface.

WallBounce.name: Wall Bounce
WallBounce.description: Factor at which objects hitting walls of this terrain type will lose speed.

AllowRespawn.name: Allow Respawn
AllowRespawn.description: Determines whether objects are allowed spawn on surfaces of this terrain type.

Solid.name: Solid
Solid.description: Determines whether elements of this terrain type are solid or not.  Non-solid terrain types are used for things that allow objects to pass through it such as water.

OnTouch.name: Action on Touch
OnTouch.description: Action to perform when surfaces of this terrain type are touched by any object.

OnImpact.name: Action on Impact
OnImpact.description: Action to perform when surfaces of this terrain type are fell onto by any object.

AppearanceSurface.name: Surface Appearance
AppearanceSurface.description: Pattern to visually represent surfaces of this terrain type.

AppearanceWallNorth.name: North Wall Appearance
AppearanceWallNorth.description: Pattern to visually represent north-facing walls of this terrain type.

AppearanceWallSouth.name: South Wall Appearance
AppearanceWallSouth.description: Pattern to visually represent south-facing walls of this terrain type.

AppearanceWallWest.name: West Wall Appearance
AppearanceWallWest.description: Pattern to visually represent west-facing walls of this terrain type.

AppearanceWallEast.name: East Wall Appearance
AppearanceWallEast.description: Pattern to visually represent east-facing walls of this terrain type.

TerrainState.description: Terrain states are used for controlling the presence of individual terrain elements via the definition of conditions that include one or more of these states.  Terrain states are also exposed as boolean values in order that they can also be used to control other things, such as lifts.

State.name: Initial State
State.description: Initial value of this terrain state.

Hint.name: Hint Action
Hint.description: Action to be performed that provides a hint for this terrain state.

IconScale.name: Icon Scale
IconScale.description: Scale of the icon representing this terrain state.

ThemeSet.description: Each theme set contains multiple themes, where each theme contains the same set of elements (textures, colours, etc.), but the appearance of each element varies in each theme.  A theme set may be applied to a zone type, such that each zone of that type applies a theme from the set.  This is useful for setting different colours for each room.

TextureElements.name: Texture Elements
TextureElements.description: Texture elements of each theme in this set.

TextureElement.name: Texture Element
TextureElement.description: A texture elements of each theme in this set.

TextureElementAdd.name: Texture Element
TextureElementAdd.description: Add a texture element for each theme in this set.

ColourElements.name: Colour Elements
ColourElements.description: Colour elements of each theme in this set.

ColourElement.name: Colour Element
ColourElement.description: A colour elements of each theme in this set.

ColourElementAdd.name: Colour Element
ColourElementAdd.description: Add a colour elements for each theme in this set.

Theme.name: Themes
Theme.description: An available themes within this set.

ThemeAdd.name: Themes
ThemeAdd.description: Add a themes to this set.

ThemeName.name: Name
ThemeName.description: Name of the theme within this set.

Top.description: Models in the style of the C64 Spindizzy Top craft.

Top.name: Top Colour
Top.description: Colour of the top of this model.

Side.name: Side Colour
Side.description: Colour of the sides of this model.

Outline.name: Outline Colour
Outline.description: Colour of the outline around the edges of this model.

World.description: Worlds in which an actual game takes place.  Each world consists of one or more zones (or rooms), each of which can contain elements for the player to traverse.  Note that worlds can also be used for purposes other than gameplay.  For example, worlds can also be used to construct HUD icons, such as the compass, clue, and switch states at the top of the screen in Spindizzy.

Gravity.name: Gravity
Gravity.description: The rate at which falling objects increase their fall speed.

SlopeEffect.name: Slope Effect
SlopeEffect.description: The rate at which objects on slopes increase speed in the downward direction of the slope.  This rate is set on the basis of a slope of a single block step in height.

BounceTime.name: Bounce Control
BounceTime.description: A simulated time window in which the movement control is allowed for an object during a bounce from a surface.  This allows a small degree of control while continuously bouncing on surfaces.

Content.name: Content
Content.description: Edit the content of this world.

Editing.name: Editor Settings
Editing.description: Customize the editing functionality of this world.  Note these properties have no impact on the runtime behaviour of this world.

EditingAdvancedProperties.name: Advanced Object Properties
EditingAdvancedProperties.description: Determines whether additional advanced properties of various elements are exposed in the editor.  When disabled, only properties necessary for the functionality of the original Spindizzy are exposed.  When enabled, extra properties are shown that expose additional functionality not found in the original Spindizzy, such as lift movement speed, lift pause times, invisible terrain, ghost terrain, etc.

EditingAutomaticZoneManagement.name: Automatic Zone Management
EditingAutomaticZoneManagement.description: Type of zone to use for automatic zone management.  If no type of zone is selected, then automatic zone management is disabled for this world and zones must be drawn manually.

EditingDefaultZoneWidth.name: Default Zone Width
EditingDefaultZoneWidth.description: Width in surface tiles of automatically created zones (west to east).

EditingDefaultZoneLength.name: Default Zone Length
EditingDefaultZoneLength.description: Length in surface tiles of automatically created zones (south to north).

EditingDefaultZoneHeight.name: Default Zone Height
EditingDefaultZoneHeight.description: Height in block steps of automatically created zones (bottom to top).

EditingDefaultThemeSet.name: Default Theme Set
EditingDefaultThemeSet.description: Theme set applied to automatically created zones.

EditingBoundaryWest.name: West Boundary
EditingBoundaryWest.description: Maximum distance in surface tiles to which this world can extend westward.

EditingBoundaryEast.name: East Boundary
EditingBoundaryEast.description: Maximum distance in surface tiles to which this world can extend eastward.

EditingBoundarySouth.name: South Boundary
EditingBoundarySouth.description: Maximum distance in surface tiles to which this world can extend southward.

EditingBoundaryNorth.name: North Boundary
EditingBoundaryNorth.description: Maximum distance in surface tiles to which this world can extend northward.

EditingBoundaryDepth.name: Depth Boundary
EditingBoundaryDepth.description: Maximum distance in block steps to which this world can extend downward.

EditingBoundaryHeight.name: Height Boundary
EditingBoundaryHeight.description: Maximum distance in block steps to which this world can extend upward.

EditingDefaultEditingTool.name: Default Editing Tool
EditingDefaultEditingTool.description: The editor tool selected by default when editing the content of this world.

EditingEditingTool.name: Editing Tool
EditingEditingTool.description: A tool to be used for editing this world.

EditingEditingToolAdd.name: Editing Tool
EditingEditingToolAdd.description: Add a tool to be used for editing this world.

WorldView.description: Views of worlds that can be presented on the screen.

World.name: World
World.description: The world to be display by this view.

Camera.name: Camera
Camera.description: The camera through which to view the world.

ZoneViewType.name: ZoneViewType
ZoneViewType.description: Determine how zones are visually represented within this world.

Zoom.name: Zoom
Zoom.description: A factor by which the world in this view is zoomed.

Zone.description: Different types of rooms that can be used within a world.

ZoneObject.description: Object types with behaviour that can be customized via the application of various "traits".  This is an experimental type of resource that implements an Entity-Component-System design.  It is intended to replace the existing fixed object types, such as lifts, aliens, pick-ups, etc. with a single resource type that includes enough flexibility to cover all of these functionalities and much more.  This resource type is experimental and unused in the existing game.
